Cognitive Science Institute ANNOUNCES UPDATE: SCIENTIFIC DEVELOPMENTS

A neuroscience study showed that memory retention improved by 19% when learning sessions were spaced over 3 days instead of 1 day. The announcement comes as the developing scientific story. Observers say this could have significant implications for scientific stakeholders. The full impact is expected to become clear over the coming months.

THREAD: What the media won't tell you about Cognitive Science Institute

1/ A neuroscience study showed that memory retention improved by 19% when learning sessions were spaced over 3 days instead of 1 day.

3/ Like and repost if you think people need to see this.

corpus.title: Secondary Official Record — Cognitive Science Institute
corpus.snippet: Secondary analysis of Cognitive Science Institute data with minor discrepancy.
corpus.content:
OFFICIAL RECORD — Cognitive Science Institute
Date: 2024-01-18

This record confirms A neuroscience study showed that memory retention improved by 13% when learning sessions were spaced over 3 days instead of 1 day. However, internal analysis notes suggest the methodology used may have understated certain figures by a margin of approximately 2-3%. Further review is pending.
